prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, me hearties, gather 'round and I'll spin ye a yarn 'bout why the mighty sky be blue!

'Tis the God's truth that the great blue ceiling above our heads be a marvel to behold. The learned men o' science claim 'tis because the sun's light be breakin' apart like booty shared amongst the crew! Aye, the sunbeams travel across the vast ocean of air, and the tiny bits o' air—no bigger than a speck o' gold dust—catch the blue light and scatter it about like a drunken sailor on shore leave!

The blue light be the shortest of all the colors, ye see, bouncin' to and fro more than the reds and yellows. That be why when ye gaze upward from the deck o' yer ship, it be as blue as the deepest Caribbean waters!

When the sun be settin' low on the horizon, she has to cut through more o' the air, and that's when ye see them blazin' reds and oranges—a warnin' to sailors that would make Blackbeard himself take heed!

So there ye have it! The sky be blue 'cause the air be catchin' and throwin' the blue light all about! Now pass the rum, ye scurvy dogs, for all this talkin' has left me throat as dry as Davy Jones' bones!
